To take this step, the two companies will invest $5 billion over the following years to roll out frontier‑scale AI models, tooling, and a global engineering organization dedicated to open-source security.

Boosting open-source code security At the heart of Project Lightwell is a new operational model that bridges the gap between enterprises and the upstream communities that build the software they rely on.

Since Anthropic's Mythos Preview model has already identified nearly 3,900 serious security vulnerabilities in open-source software in just a few weeks, the urgent need for faster fixes is crystal clear.

article_items.title: Meta, other social networks will pay $27 million to settle Kentucky school district lawsuit - Engadget
article_items.description: The Kentucky school district that filed a social media addiction lawsuit against Meta and other companies is getting $27 million in settlement.
article_items.excerpt: The Kentucky school district that filed a social media addiction lawsuit against Meta and other companies is getting $27 million in settlement.
article_items.summary: Some of the companies announced reaching an agreement with the plaintiff earlier this month but didn't reveal the financial details of their settlement. The companies that settled the social media addiction lawsuit filed by the Breathitt County school district in Kentucky will pay a collective amount of around $27 million, according to Bloomberg. YouTube will also provide the district's teachers with training programs that would help them use its platform for their classes more effectively. The Kentucky school district that filed a social media addiction lawsuit against Meta and other companies is getting $27 million in settlement. Since the defendants have settled this case, the first actual social media addiction trial involving a school district could happen next year in February 2027. key_points: The companies that settled the social media addiction lawsuit filed by the Breathitt County school district in Kentucky will pay a collective amount of around $27 million, according to Bloomberg.
Some of the companies announced reaching an agreement with the plaintiff earlier this month but didn't reveal the financial details of their settlement.
The Kentucky school district that filed a social media addiction lawsuit against Meta and other companies is getting $27 million in settlement.
Bloomberg says Meta is paying the district $9 million, more than what any of the other defendants are paying.

Breathitt County's lawsuit is just one of the more than 1,300 complaints filed by school districts around the US, accusing the companies of creating products as addictive as cigarettes and then targeting minors.
The Kentucky school district was originally asking for $60 million to finance its mental health programs so that it could address the harms of social media on its students.
Since the defendants have settled this case, the first actual social media addiction trial involving a school district could happen next year in February 2027.
Meta, other social networks will pay $27 million to settle Kentucky school district lawsuit Over 1,000 school districts in the US sued social media companies over 'addictive' products.
Bloomberg says the lawsuits could cost the companies $400 billion in all - Breathitt County is a small rural district, after all, and bigger ones could get larger payouts.
Snap and TikTok agreed to settle for $8 million each, while YouTube negotiated a payment of a bit more than $2 million.
Its hearing was scheduled for June 12.
YouTube will also provide the district's teachers with training programs that would help them use its platform for their classes more effectively.

article_items.title: Meta has struggled at selling anything other than ads. Will AI be different?
article_items.description: Meta is making a major push to expand its business beyond online advertising, but past efforts show that success is far from guaranteed.
article_items.excerpt: Meta is making a major push to expand its business beyond online advertising, but past efforts show that success is far from guaranteed.
article_items.summary: The Meta AI subscriptions announced this week will cost $7.99 and $19.99 a month, depending on certain features and capabilities. In April, Meta raised its 2026 guidance for AI-related capital expenditures to between $125 billion and $145 billion, up from a prior range of $115 billion to $135 billion. Those paid offerings, available first in Singapore, Guatemala and Bolivia, coincide with the official release of premium subscription plans for Instagram, Facebook and WhatsApp, and higher-tier versions of its verification subscription service, which is designed to help businesses protect their brand. The company is trying to capitalize on the surprise success of the Ray-Ban Meta glasses, in partnership with.
